transcript_text:
spk_0: Okay, so tell me a bit about yourself. What are you currently doing? What course you're in, what year...? Yeah, just about that.
spk_1: I'm a first-year in Bath, and I'm studying mechanical engineering with management and manufacturing. Yeah... yeah... no, no, no.
spk_0: What were you gonna say?
spk_1: Oh, I don't know... if you want me to say a bit more... more about myself.
spk_0: You can say it if you want to say anything more... but that was perfect!
spk_1: No, no... it's okay. It's okay.
spk_0: Okay, so what does a typical week look like for you? Like, contact hours versus, like, independent study... So, how many hours of lectures versus how many hours you, like, study independently?
spk_1: I think in a week I have roughly 20 contact hours and then independent study. I would say probably 10... like, two hours every day and not much on the weekends. And then on most weekdays, I stay in... like, have dinner in my flat and cook it myself. And then on weekends, I'll sometimes have dinner out or order or... yeah, okay, nice!
spk_0: So, tell me a bit more about your timetable structure... like busy days versus lighter days. Maybe in, like, uni or, like, even weekends.
spk_1: I have... My Mondays are really heavy days. I have sometimes seven hours on a Monday, but then my Tuesdays and Wednesdays are very light. I only have an hour on each of those days. And then Thursdays and Fridays, like three hours, which is not bad compared to my Monday. But yeah... and then my weekends are very chill. I don't have any, like, sports commitments or anything, so it's kind of just working on my own schedule if I have plans or not.
spk_0: Okay, I was gonna ask you about that... Are you in any extracurriculars, like sports, societies, or, like, any work?
spk_1: I mean, I'm in a... like, an engineering one. I'm in Team Bath Heart, which is like... okay, that creates a mechanical heart. It's like this whole big project with engineering and bio and comp sci and all of that. Nice!
spk_0: Okay, so... I mean, not 'who do you live with,' but, like, 'how many people do you live with?' And because I know you live in quads, right? Student con... So, like, that's, like, 10 people. Is that right? Okay.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: And who usually makes decisions around, like, food... like, in your flat? Like, do you eat with... do you cook together or, like, separately?
spk_1: I have, like, one main flatmate that I usually coordinate with, like when our lectures are, when we'll go to the gym, and so that we try to eat around the same time because we prefer not to eat, like, completely alone. But our flat is also very social, so anytime I go in the kitchen, it's almost guaranteed I'm gonna have someone to eat with. That's just my main priority, no matter who it is... Yeah.
spk_0: Amazing! Do you do any, like, food shops or anything together?
spk_1: Yeah, a lot of times if someone wants... Because a lot of people don't want to go all the way into town. So then they'll send on the group and be like, 'I'm gonna do a Sainsbury's order or going to do a Deliveroo order. Does anyone want anything?' And we'll just do it. And then if we can get the cost high enough, then they get free delivery and stuff like that.
spk_0: Amazing! Okay, so now we're going to switch and talk about social media for a moment... So, which social media platforms do you typically use?
spk_1: Mainly TikTok, Instagram, and Snapchat.
spk_0: Okay... do you use any, like, YouTube, Facebook, or X?
spk_1: No.
spk_0: Okay, what social platforms do you use the most out of those? And if you were to, like, order them from most used to least used, how would you order it?
spk_1: I'd probably say Snapchat, TikTok, Instagram... and like, when it's more on days that have, like, free time, then stock will go higher.
spk_0: Okay... roughly, how much time do you spend on them each day? Like, your screen time?
spk_1: Like, in total, of the three? Yeah, hopefully, like, five hours.
spk_0: Okay, nice. Where do you tend to see food-related content online?
spk_1: A lot on TikTok, I would say... always, like, searching recipes and stuff for inspo.
spk_0: Yeah, I do the same. I literally have, like, a folder of, like, that's saved. Yeah, yeah... Do you follow any food influencers or, like, creators or, like, even local food accounts, like maybe in Bath or, like, London?
spk_1: Yeah, I've been... I think I follow, like, Joan the Juice and Selena and Bath. And then, like, there's, like, a few people on TikTok that I've seen, like, making easy recipes, so I follow them. Yeah.
spk_0: Have you ever ordered food because you saw it on social media?
spk_1: Yeah, definitely.
spk_0: Do you remember what it was like, being promoted, or like, what made you, like, decide to, like, buy it?
spk_1: I think it's just like... I like, see something that looks really yummy, and in that moment, I'm really hungry, and I don't have food in the fridge, so I'll just order it... just mainly based on the appearance of the food.
spk_0: Okay, so it's the food itself, or like... would it be like an offer, maybe, or like a promotion that you saw?
spk_1: Oh, yeah.
spk_0: Or like.
spk_1: Yeah, like, sometimes because I have delivery on Uber Eats, and if I get, like, a notification and it's like, oh, like a promotion... I'll always go look at what the promotion is. If it's worth it, I'll order. Definitely!
spk_0: Okay, can you walk me through, like, a typical weekday from morning to night? What do you eat, when, and where?
spk_1: Yeah. Okay. So I usually, like, when I wake up, depending on the day... but around, like, 10-ish, I'll have, like, either oats or cereal or yogurt—something small in my dorm, just like as my breakfast. And I'll usually... then I'll buy, like, at any of the cafes on campus and get a coffee or something with, like, my student credit so that I don't actually pay for it, and then go to my lectures. And then, if it's a long day, I'll get, like, a... like a granola bar or something in between. And then around, like, maybe four, I'll have, like, a late lunch because my dinners are usually also quite late. So I'll have, like, a bagel or something. And I usually make that at home, but sometimes I'll buy it from a cafe on campus, and then I'll either go work out or do my work or something. And then I'll usually cook dinner at around 8 and eat at, like, around 9, but also in my dorm. Like, this is, like, on a weekday. Weekdays is usually everything in my dorm or from campus, sort of. Okay.
spk_0: Amazing! How does this differ on the weekends?
spk_1: So, on the weekends, I feel like at least once during the weekend I'll have dinner. Either I'll go out to a restaurant in town, or I'll order with my friends. But also, I feel like my breakfast and lunch kind of merge into one on the weekends because I'm waking up a lot later... Yeah. And then also, like if I'm out super late, I might grab a really late snack in town later on.
spk_0: Okay, nice. What do you look for when deciding what to eat, like during the weekdays or, like, weekends? Just in general...
spk_1: I think I usually try to have, like, a protein in all of my meals. So that's, like, kind of... I base each one of my meals around, like, I'll be like, I have chicken, so I'm gonna make, like, a chicken-based lunch or a beef-based dinner. That's, like, my most important thing. But yeah, I kind of... I don't love eating the same thing over and over, so I kind of try to have ingredients that I can make in a bunch of ways because I tried the whole meal-prepping route, and then I just got so, like, redundant. I was like, I can't keep eating the same thing over and over. Yeah!
spk_0: So, on that... actually, how often do you cook?
spk_1: How often do I cook?
spk_0: Yeah,
spk_1: Probably every day, except not much on the weekends. But yeah, like, every dinner is basically cooked right before I eat it. It's very rare that I'll make it from the day before.
spk_0: Okay... what does cooking usually mean for you?
spk_1: Honestly, like, I didn't cook until I came to uni. It's definitely not a passion of mine at all. Cooking means I'm so hungry, I just want to eat. But other than that... nothing. I don't really get creative with my meals. I don't do anything special. It's just enough to, like, fulfill me.
spk_0: Nice. Okay. Do you like... would you see it as something like from scratch, or like something pre-prepared that you heat up? Is it like chopping?
spk_1: Yeah, like chopping vegetables, and like I'll, like, season my meat and, like, cook and stuff. I... I don't really get, like, pre-prepped meals and stuff, heat them up. I'll make it, but it won't be, like, some difficult, extravagant recipe, or I won't, like, bake for fun or anything. Like, I'm cooking because I have to eat.
spk_0: Which meals do you think feel the most stressful, and which meals feel the most enjoyable?
spk_1: I think my breakfast is probably, like, the least enjoyable for me because I just don't really like breakfast foods. And I'm like... I know I need to eat, and, like, my stomach is really hungry and stuff, but this is not what I want to eat right now. And so I kind of just, like, don't enjoy that meal much. Whereas, like, dinner, it's like... okay, I feel like I have... like my day is basically done. I can kind of choose along. I can, like, relax with this meal. I can eat it for so long because... because I like to eat slowly. And so having that with dinner is, like... I like that. So I definitely enjoy my dinners a lot more than, like, breakfasts and my lunches because lunches also... it's like in between lectures, and I'm kind of, like, stressed and just, like, I need to make something quickly to eat and then get out, and, like, I don't actually get to, like, sit there and, like, digest my food and stuff. Yeah.
spk_0: Okay... when do you think food feels functional? Social versus comforting?
spk_1: I think 'functional' is like... as a... like breakfast and lunch is like 90% of the time functional because it's also usually alone. And I think that's like a big difference. When I'm eating with people, then it's like more social, and then with family or like a food that I had a lot at home, that's a lot more comfort. Yeah, I think like especially now, like with fasting, Ramadan, like having the meals... meals like from home and eating with people from my culture... that's more like comfort. Yeah, yeah.
spk_0: And what about groceries? How often do you typically do, like, your grocery shopping?
spk_1: So, at the beginning of uni, I would do like a weekly food shop, but now it's kind of like I'll do a really big food shop, and then for the rest of the month, basically, I'm not really going and doing another big food shop. I'm just picking up things as they run out... like I need this, I'll go grab this. But I won't do a big shop except for maybe once a month.
spk_0: Okay, and would this be like shopping? Would it be like solo or...? I think you mentioned in the beginning, you said you do like group ordering sometimes with, like, the flat. Someone would, like, message on the group chat. Would you guys order, like, groceries together? Would it usually just be, like, a big shop, like you said, alone and then with, like, small top-up shops in between?
spk_1: So we'll do like the big grocery thing together, but it's... it's like split up into like what each person needs. Like, we don't, like, share our food or anything. It's just... ask what they need.
spk_0: Yeah.
spk_1: But then, even sometimes, like, I'll do that when I really need a lot of things. But sometimes, when they're doing a big shop, I don't need a lot of things... I'll still add just, like, one thing or a few things. Or if I'm in town, I'll, like... I'll pick up something quickly. Okay.
spk_0: Roughly, how much do you spend on food in a typical week?
spk_1: Just on, like, groceries or also, like, if I'm gonna eat out or something... just everything.
spk_0: Like food delivery, groceries, and... like, also eating out.
spk_1: Probably like 60-ish pounds, maybe a bit more... sometimes a bit less, sometimes.
spk_0: Do you have a set budget, like, each week, or does it change?
spk_1: No, not really. Like, roughly, I'm like, oh, if I'm gonna do... if I'm doing like a weekly grocery shop, I, in my mind, I'm like, oh, it should be around 40, but sometimes it's a lot more than that. Just depends what I have... Yeah. Okay.
spk_0: If you would split it, like, between, like, food delivery, groceries, and eating out from, like, the 60-ish budget range... like, how much would you say, like, each, like, thing is?
spk_1: I think, like, food delivery is probably my least... Like, I'll... It'll be like we ordered a bagel; it'll be like 5 to 10 pounds, and then groceries will be like 30 to 40. Especially on the weeks where I'm, like, not eating a lot, it'll be closer to, like, 30, and then eating out's like 20. Okay,
spk_0: So, you said you don't really order, like, food delivery as much... like, the budget, as in, like, in the typical week, is not put into that as much.
spk_1: It'll be like... I will order food delivery, as in grocery delivery, but not food as in, like, ready food, like from Ready or something. Okay.
spk_0: What days of the week, though? If you were, like, to order, like, food delivery, like, what reason would you, like, order food delivery for? Like, which days of the week are you most likely to order food?
spk_1: I think Sunday and Monday are, like, my most likely days because that's usually when... like, Sunday... like, the grocery stores are closed. So if I, like... they close early, so if I need something and I can't pick it up, I'll order something. But also a lot of things close early, so it's a bit difficult. And then Mondays as well, because I haven't done my food shop yet or haven't picked up the stuff, and then I have a really busy day on Monday. So then I'm like, okay, I might just order something today. I feel like those are my two biggest days. Yeah!
spk_0: Do you order it, like, usually, mostly, like, alone or, like, individually? Or would it be, like, a group thing with the flat?
spk_1: Probably alone. Or like, if I'm having dinner with, like, a few friends, sometimes we'll... We've done, like, oh, like, we're each gonna, like, order something for the other person and stuff like that.
spk_0: Is this like a grocery shop or food... like, like restaurant food?
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Okay, dude.
spk_1: Nice. Okay.
spk_0: Does delivery feel like a tree, a habit, or just a part of your normal spending?
spk_1: What?
spk_0: Delivery... does delivery feel like a treat, a habit, or just a part of your normal spending?
spk_1: I would say it's a part of my normal spending, but, like, it is a treat... Like, I do really like delivery over going somewhere. But that's why it's part of my normal spending, because I think it's worth it.
spk_0: Okay, sorry... I didn't quite catch that because you kind of, like, froze a bit.
spk_1: Okay. It's like normal spending, but delivery is like a treat. Like, I do appreciate big things delivered to me over going to get them. But that's why I count it as part of spending, because I see the value in it, I guess... Yeah.
spk_0: Can you tell me about the last time you ordered delivery? Like, what triggered it? Who were you with, and what was going on that day?
spk_1: Yeah, I was with my flatmate, Sophia, and I wasn't gonna get anything that day, I don't think. And then she was like, I have a delivery voucher, and it's gonna expire. And I was like, oh... like, yeah. Like, she offered me one because she had multiple, and she didn't need them all. And she was like, oh, like, if you want to use it. And I was like, you know what? Like, yeah, sure. And, like, since I saw that the voucher... because I wasn't in need of groceries at the time, and then I saw that the voucher also applied to boots, and I was like, oh, like, I really need to use shampoo, like a body wash and a hair oil, and just, like, type of essentials that it's like, I'm gonna have to buy these anyways, and they're, like, running low, so I might as well make use of this voucher. Like, even though I wasn't planning on anything in that moment, it was like, eventually, I'm gonna need to buy these, so it's, like, worth it.
spk_0: Okay, so it was, like, a spontaneous order from the voucher from Sophia.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: But it was an individual order... Did you guys group order it, or...?
spk_1: Okay... no, individual. Okay.
spk_0: What kind of food do you typically order? Like, as in... like, food delivery, not groceries?
spk_1: I feel like it's usually not, like, big, like, dinner meals, because I... I don't know, I worry about, like, delivery and, like, the food going cold and stuff like that. So it'll be more like sandwiches or bagels and stuff like that. That is, like, easier to, like, easier to deliver and, like, difficult to mess up.
spk_0: Yeah.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Okay... are there any go-to orders, or, like, do you look for variety when you're on the food delivery apps?
spk_1: No... Yeah, I feel like I look for variety because, like, since I'm ordering anyways, I can order from any of the stuff available. So I usually just browse and, like, see what I'm in the mood for, and I'll, like, put in, like, whatever cuisine I'm in the mood for, and then look at the restaurants that come up for that cuisine.
spk_0: Do you think your ordering habits have changed over time?
spk_1: I think, yeah... I think at the beginning of the year, I was ordering a lot less. I was like, more... I can cook. I have the time. I'll go into town. But now I feel like I just... That's like time that's like more valuable, you know? I'm like, I don't want to waste it going to the grocery store, to be honest. I just want to order. Like, I don't want to cook right now. I want to chill and be able to order food instead. So yeah, definitely. Okay,
spk_0: So, you talked about how, obviously, you do more grocery deliveries than food deliveries on the delivery apps, and you also do some group grocery deliveries as well, or food deliveries with your flat. How do you decide whether to go to the shop versus ordering groceries?
spk_1: I think I usually go to the shop. I'm going into town for another reason anyways, and I'm like, I might as well go in person because I think there is a difference in the prices of, like, ordering than going in person. I feel it's a bit cheaper in person. So... so I'm going anyways. I'll go to the shop. But then, if I'm not and I don't have the time to, and it's like, well, I need the food anyways... like, I can't just put this off until I'm going to town because I need the food. So then I'll order.
spk_0: Okay... what do you think stops you from ordering groceries more often?
spk_1: I think it is just like the price difference between in-store and online. Like, I think it also varies between different websites. Like, I don't know which ones give the best deals and stuff. But just from what I've noticed, like, my food shop does come out to be a bit more expensive when I order it. But at the same time, like, one, I'm saving on, like, the, like, there-and-back bus fee, and also, like, a lot of times there is some sort of offer. So then I just...
spk_0: Yeah, okay on that, actually. So, have you seen any promos for grocery delivery, and what promo was it?
spk_1: Yeah, I mean there's like... I discovered this last week... there's like something on, like, Mondays where, like, produce is like 50% off or something, like vegetables and dairy and stuff. I didn't know this and I... It was on, like, Sainsbury's, but I think on Uber Eats, Sainsbury's and on Deliveroo, Sainsbury's. It was like that. I didn't know that! Yeah, but I also had, up until like last week, I had Uber One. So then I had, like, a bunch of promotions. I just felt it this week because I'm not in the UK, but okay, that like always gave me free delivery and stuff, and like there's always, like, extra promotions with that, that would just be, like, money off of groceries and stuff like that.
spk_0: From seeing these, like, promos for grocery delivery, did it change, like, how you, like, ordered groceries?
spk_1: I guess, yeah. Because, like, before seeing promos and stuff, I didn't order as much as I do now. But also, like, then I'm like, oh, like, when I saw that Monday thing, then I, like, I... I was in need of a lot of vegetables, one. So then I was like, oh, I'll just wait until Monday. Like, it was Saturday. I was like, oh, order this on Monday. It'll be, like, half price! Like, yeah, so it definitely does change.
spk_0: Okay, amazing! When do you decide not to order delivery?
spk_1: Oh, I'm very... Like, I'm not someone that just, like, clicks 'order' very easily. I, like, overthink it a lot. So it'll be like, I'm, like, making my cart, and I, like, have made my whole cart. And then I'll decide not to, like, never mind, I'm not gonna order this. And then I'll just convince myself either I don't need the stuff urgently, or I can... oh, I'm going into town soon, or I don't know, like... but it'll always be, like, a lot in my... I've already made my whole cart. And then I'll just decide not to click 'order.'
spk_0: What do you think makes you decide not to click 'order'? Like, what's the trigger?
spk_1: I... because when it's a big shop, I'm like, oh, my gosh, this is so much money at once! I don't want to spend this, which is why I prefer to do little shops, because sacrificing in what I'm buying is easier when I'm buying it in, like, small doses. Because I'm like, oh, it's just a few pounds, and that's fine. So then I have issues with big shops, but at the same time, I'm probably not going to do an individual order for, like, items... okay?
spk_0: What do you think makes delivery feel worth it?
spk_1: Just the fact that it removes the hassle of going in person, but also, like, because a lot of times my flat orders on, like, Sainsbury's website, but then sometimes me and my friend will order on, like, Deliveroo or Uber. And I, like, significantly prefer that because they just come literally within half an hour. It'll be like, my groceries are at my door in half an hour. Like, with the Sainsbury's steps, you have to wait a few days. You have to, like, schedule delivery. And I'm like, this, like, I'm just sitting in my room and I want to cook... And I order it in half an hour. Everything's there. Like, it's just such a time and, like, effort saver more than anything.
spk_0: Okay, what do you think makes delivery feel indulgent... guilty or, like, justified?
spk_1: I think, like, 'justified' is, like, anytime there's an offer or special occasion... stuff like that. 'Indulgent' is, like, sometimes I, like, have food in the fridge, or I have, like, all the ingredients to make something, and I've, like, kind of, like, been like, 'Oh, tonight for dinner, I'm gonna have this.' And then a friend will be like, 'Oh, do you want to order this?' And I'm like, 'Yeah, let's just order that.' That's, like, indulgence. Like, I don't actually need to do this. And I guess, like, sometimes there's guilt in that, but since it's, like, in a social kind of aspect, the guilt doesn't, like, really come through. Guilt is just when it was, like, I have everything I need and, like, today was not a significant spending day, and I've just gone and ordered stuff that I really didn't need. Probably wouldn't have ended up buying if, like, then it's not an essential that I was going to buy anyways, or something comes and it doesn't even taste good... then the guilt. The guilt.
spk_0: But when you buy, like, for example, like you said, when you have those ingredients in your fridge, but then you still end up buying, like, other stuff... like, what would make you? What's the driver to, like, buying those other ingredients?
spk_1: Well, it'll be like... my friends will want to, like, have dinner together and then order this whole... like, these other ingredients for what they want to make together. I'm like, 'Oh, that's so fun!' And it's kind of like... I'm willing to pay for this because it's like a social... like, experience that I'm getting out of it. Yeah, it's usually just, like, social pressure that will make me do, like, these indulgent things. Yeah. Amazing.
spk_0: Just circling back to, like, food delivery apps in general, because you said you do use Uber Eats and Deliveroo, but which food delivery apps, like, in general, do you use? Like, other than these two... or which do you use most often?
spk_1: I use probably Uber Eats the most often because... I don't know, it was like, I started using it. I didn't really know about the room; I just continued using it. And then, like, I discovered Deliveroo, and I realized there's like... sometimes I want to order something, and it's only on Deliveroo and not on Uber Eats. In that case, I'll go to Deliveroo, but usually, if the thing is on both, I order on Uber Eats, especially now that I have the Uber One discount, because then I'll get, like, free delivery and all these sorts of promotions and stuff. Okay!
spk_0: Have you seen anything from food delivery apps that stood out to you recently?
spk_1: No, I don't think so... I think it's specific.
spk_0: What tends to matter most in the moment when you order? I'll say a list. You can tell me to repeat it again because there are a few, but... So, what tends to matter most in the moment when you order? Would it be price, offers and promotions, speed, habit, or subscription benefits?
spk_1: Oh, that's really difficult. What was, like, the fourth one?
spk_0: So, price offers and promotions... speed... habit... Habits is the fourth one.
spk_1: Okay. I would say, like: price, offers, speed, subscription, benefits... and then habit.
spk_0: Okay. Now, can you rate how well Deliveroo and Uber Eats provide those on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 is extremely terrible and 5 is extremely good? So, firstly, for price, deliver out of five...
spk_1: Four or three...? Three to four.
spk_0: Three to four... Okay, Uber Eats, a four. What about for offers and promotions for delivery on Uber Eats, out of five?
spk_1: I would say four for Deliveroo, and a five for Uber Eats.
spk_0: Okay, Speed,
spk_1: Like a five for Deliveroo... I've had a really good experience with that. And then a four for Uber Eats.
spk_0: Okay... what about habit?
spk_1: I guess, like, a 3 per Deliveroo... and the 4 reads.
spk_0: And lastly... subscription benefits!
spk_1: I mean, I would give Uber Eats a five because I, like, had a subscription, so I, like, know it better. I don't really know for Delivery. So, like, 3 or 4... But I don't really know. I haven't had a subscription there.
spk_0: No worries. Okay. Okay, so I'm going to give you a little scenario... or maybe like something... This probably might have happened to you already before. But, for example, if an app has the restaurant you want, but another app is cheaper, what do you usually do in this situation?
spk_1: Is the other app that's cheaper... having the same restaurant?
spk_0: Yeah, same restaurant... like, same food, but another app is cheaper. Like, for example, you're looking to buy this dish, and on Deliveroo, it was 2 pounds cheaper. But on Uber Eats, it was like just the normal price. So, what do you usually do? Like, in this situation, I usually would...
spk_1: Go for just the cheaper one, even if it's not like my... the one that I regularly use. Because I'm kind of just thinking of, in the moment, what's better.
spk_0: Yeah.
spk_1: Yeah. Okay.
spk_0: And is there, like, a certain, like, threshold, like, of how much it would be for you to, like, change ups?
spk_1: Yeah, I mean, if it's like a big order, like groceries or something, and it's like a couple pounds difference, then I'm quite indifferent. But especially on smaller orders where a few pounds is a bigger, bigger percentage of the overall cost... in that case, I'll switch. Yeah. But I'll also look at other things, like if the delivery is the same... like the speed of delivery and stuff, because I don't want to pay less but then wait an extra two hours or something!
spk_0: Okay, so how much effort do you think you're, like, willing to put into saving money at that moment? And how much would the amount be for you to change?
